name: Nate Pennell's Mulligan Stew with Blueberry D
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp mild vegetable oil, such as safflower
1 medium rutabaga (peeled and cut into 1/2 inch cubes)
1 large potato (peeled and cut into 2 inch cubes)
4 small carrots (peeled and cut into 1/2 inch cubes)
1/2 cup wild blueberries (fresh or frozen, or 1/4 cup golden raisins coarsely chopped)
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse chicken well and pat thoroughly dry.
2. Heat butter and oil in a large (at least 8 quart) heavy stockpot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
3. When hot, add chicken pieces, season generously with salt and pepper, and brown on both sides, seasoning the other side when you turn the pieces. Brown in several batches to avoid crowding the pan; about 8 minutes per batch.
4. Transfer chicken to a plate or bowl, drain off half the fat in the pan, and return the chicken to the pan. Add enough water to just cover the chicken. Bring to a boil.
5. Add herbs and rutabaga. Return to boil. Cook until rutabaga is nearly soft but still crisp, about 10 minutes.
6. Add potato and carrots; cook until carrots are nearly tender, about 15 minutes.
7. While stew cooks, make dumpling dough: Sift flour, baking powder, 1 tsp salt, baking soda, and nutmeg into a medium bowl. Make a well in the center and add egg, melted butter, and buttermilk. Mix together with a small whisk, fork, or fingers to form a fairly stiff dough. Fold in blueberries or raisins.
8. Adjust stew seasoning; ensure it is boiling vigorously. Stir in peas and parsnips.
9. Drop heaping tablespoonfuls of dumpling dough on top of the stew so dumplings do not touch. Cover and cook until dumplings are puffed and cooked through, no more than 15 minutes, checking occasionally to prevent overcooking and drying.
10. To serve, cut through dumplings with a spoon and ladle chicken and vegetables into warmed shallow soup bowls (remove thyme and bay leaves). Place dumplings on top; serve immediately.
11. Pinot Noir pairs well with this stew.
